message:
fix(orphan_reaper): skip value-taking wezterm flags before the subcommand

Fixes ft-ywlpu. The orphan reaper's `parse_ps_line_if_reapable`
walked tokens after `cli` and treated the first non-flag token as
the subcommand name. That shape works for `wezterm cli list` but
rejects legitimate invocations like
`wezterm cli --config-file /tmp/x.toml list` — the `/tmp/x.toml`
flag-value looked like a subcommand and failed to match
`REAPABLE_SUBCOMMANDS`, so the process became unreapable. Once
FrankenTerm or operators add value-taking CLI flags before a real
subcommand, this silently dropped short-lived helper processes off
the reaper's radar.

## Fix

Added a conservative allowlist of flags known to take a separate
value token before the subcommand:

    const PRE_SUBCOMMAND_VALUE_FLAGS: &[&str] =
        &["--config-file", "--config", "--class"];

Parser now walks tokens with three rules:

1. `--flag=value` forms — skip the whole token; it carries its
   own value.
2. Bare `--flag` that matches `PRE_SUBCOMMAND_VALUE_FLAGS` — skip
   the flag AND the next token (the value).
3. Any other bare `--flag` (booleans like `--prefer-mux`) — skip
   just the flag.

First remaining non-flag token is matched against
`REAPABLE_SUBCOMMANDS`. This preserves the allowlist-driven safety
contract: unknown flag shapes still rejects the line rather than
falling through to an unsafe kill path.

## Scope of the allowlist

Conservatively enumerated from the shipped wezterm CLI surface
only. Unknown or future flags will cause the parser to reject
(line does not match REAPABLE_SUBCOMMANDS), which is the safe
failure mode — an unreapable process will just linger a bit
longer until it gets cleaned up by another mechanism. Adding a
flag is a one-line change here plus a regression test.

## Reformatting

The surrounding `run_orphan_reaper` body was reindented one level
deeper to sit cleanly inside the
`#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` gate it already lives
under. No logic change in that block — git diff shows 83 added
lines but ~50 of those are pure reindentation of existing code.
Separating the reformat from the parser fix would have meant
three commits instead of two for a mechanical-and-visible change,
which didn't seem worth the bisect granularity trade.

## Tests

Three new regression cases at the bottom of the tests module,
each feeding a realistic `ps` line:

- `accepts_with_config_file_flag_after_cli_before_subcommand` —
  the exact reproducer from the bead description.
- `accepts_with_config_override_flag_after_cli_before_subcommand`
  — the short-form `--config` variant.
- `accepts_with_class_flag_after_cli_before_subcommand` —
  the `--class` variant, important on Linux/X11 setups where it
  shows up in persisted wezterm launchers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
